definition: Crowding-out of private investment by government borrowing appears to have vanished in post-2008 data: partial correlation dropped from -0.38 (pre-2008, p<0.01) to -0.12 (post-2008, p=0.31). Whether crowding-out re-emerges as QT progresses is a critical open question.
evidence: Structural break coincides with QE, global savings expansion, excess reserves
Excluding QE periods leaves N~15 observations — too few for inference
open_questions: Is the disappearance structural or an artifact of QE and short samples?
Will QT and narrowing global savings surplus re-activate the channel?
Does the fiscal supply channel operate through term premium rather than traditional IS-LM crowding-out?
